question: Model an `Order` class and a `Customer` class. An `Order` should have an `orderID` and a reference or pointer to the `Customer` who placed it, demonstrating an association relationship where objects can reference each other without strict ownership.

test_cases.expected:
Customer Alice (ID: 1) created.
Customer Bob (ID: 2) created.
Order 101 created.
Order 102 created.
--- Initial display ---
Order ID: 101, Placed by: Customer ID: 1, Name: Alice
Order ID: 102, Placed by: Customer ID: 2, Name: Bob
--- Resetting customerA shared_ptr (simulating customer deletion) ---
Customer Alice (ID: 1) destroyed.
--- Display after customerA deletion ---
Order ID: 101, Placed by: Unknown Customer (deleted)
Order ID: 102, Placed by: Customer ID: 2, Name: Bob
Order 102 destroyed.
Order 101 destroyed.
Customer Bob (ID: 2) destroyed.
